items.briefing:
Australian cyber experts are flagging a rise in third-party supplier risks, warning organisations to scrutinise who has access to their systems and data. Separately, Australians are being warned they're particularly vulnerable to scams during major life events such as buying a home, getting married, or starting a new job — times when people are more likely to act quickly without checking carefully. Meanwhile, a recent study found many office workers are using AI tools in ways that break their organisation's policies, raising data security concerns.

On a positive note, research suggests everyday Australians face reduced cybercrime risk overall, though small businesses are increasingly in the crosshairs. **Practical tip:** Before sharing any sensitive information — especially during a big life change — pause and independently verify who you're actually dealing with.

featured.title: Australian cyber experts warn of third-party risk surge
featured.quote: Weak vendor oversight exposes Australian schools and businesses to significant data breach risks.
